summary: Import-time execution of obfuscated AES-decrypted payload via new Function()
details: On require()/import, index.js immediately invokes pkg.run(), which AES-256-GCM-decrypts a bundled ciphertext (DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT in src/config/defaults.js) using a hardcoded passphrase ('default-dev-passphrase') and salt ('encode-npm-c-salt'), passes the result through two custom codec layers (position-unit-codec, encode-decode-codec — both controlled by the same author), and evaluates the decoded string with `new Function(String(decoded))()` (src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js:50,:58). Shipping the ciphertext together with the key needed to decrypt it provides no confidentiality — the encryption exists solely to defeat static review of an opaque executable payload that runs on every consumer of the package. The README's framing as a generic 'AES utility' is a cover story: legitimate AES libraries do not auto-evaluate a built-in ciphertext on import. Package metadata reinforces the picture: empty author field, no homepage, boilerplate license, and dependency on two co-authored codec packages that can mutate the decoded payload across versions.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
